sourceMovie.title: The Wailing
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Wailing (2016) stuck because it commits to its horror premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Na Hong-jin's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The horror and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Weapons
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Wailing clicked for you, Weapons (2025) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and mystery, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Wailing?
faq.answer: Try Weapons, The Mothman Prophecies, Exhuma—each shares DNA with The Wailing while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Wailing?
faq.answer: The Wailing (2016) is best described as Horror, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Wailing worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
